$description: Citron typography v2 — the brand voice. Space Grotesk carries the display identity (engineered geometry, product-lab confidence); IBM Plex Sans carries body and UI (rational, warm, endlessly legible); IBM Plex Mono carries code and data. Fonts ship self-hosted in the package — import '@citron-systems/citron-ds/fonts'.

typography.$description: Brand font stack — geometric display over rational body, one engineered voice

sans.$value: 'IBM Plex Sans', 'SF Pro Text', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif
sans.$description: UI and body — IBM Plex Sans, rational and warm. Self-hosted variable font (wght 100–700)

mono.$value: 'IBM Plex Mono', 'SF Mono', ui-monospace, Menlo, Consolas, monospace
mono.$description: Code, data, tabular numerals — IBM Plex Mono, same superfamily as body

display.$value: 'Space Grotesk', 'IBM Plex Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif
display.$description: Headlines and brand moments — Space Grotesk, engineered geometry with personality. Self-hosted variable font (wght 300–700)

fontSize.$description: Dual-register scale: restrained UI sizes (xs–2xl) plus a display register (3xl–6xl) with real presence for brand moments

3xl.$description: 40px — display entry point, Space Grotesk territory

6xl.$description: 88px — maximum display, specimen and brand moments only

tighter.$description: Statement display — Space Grotesk needs less negative tracking than Inter did

wider.$description: Uppercase meta, eyebrow labels — the Citron editorial signature

fontFeatures.$description: OpenType feature settings — part of the brand voice

display.$description: Space Grotesk alternates — single-storey a, simplified forms at display sizes

numeric.$description: Tabular figures + slashed zero for data, tables, and metrics
